item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Saw that this article went through some revisions (mainly the removal of that terrible redaction) so I decided to reread it. Reread it, enjoyed it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And it's a heave-ho, hi-ho, comin' down the plains
Stealin' wheat and barley and all the other grains
It's a ho-hey, hi-hey farmers bar yer doors
When ya see the Jolly Roger on Regina's mighty shores

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with all of stormbreath’s points above. Other than some tone issues, I think this article is solid.

Any humans found by SCP-4105-B instances will be violently [REDACTED]

I find these kinds of redactions to be pointless, and they come across as cliche shock value rather than something a researcher would actually redact. There are only so many things non-intelligent water beings can violently do to someone, and I think the article would have more impact if it just spelled things out.

Novote for now, but that could change with tweaks.

Screw it, upvote because I liked reading it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly? I like this, but it could use more work. Some suggestions:
– hide block
  • Clarification in the first paragraph that the event is not annual, and then possibly some more information about how many times it has happened within the past X years.
  • Changing "although it has been theorized that the mistreatment of indigenous groups who have lived near SCP-4105-A correlates with SCP-4105 events" to "a correlation between SCP-4105 events and the mistreatment of indigenous groups living near SCP-4105-A."
    • This change has a near-identical meaning, but is phrased in a less expository fashion and reads better.
  • The second paragraph could be restructured. I would recommend adding more events that occur, rather than just the two. Additionally, moving some of the initial warning signs into the description would be prudent.
  • REDACTING what the 4105-B instances do to people is a case of lazy and pointless expungement. Either put something there or just remove it altogether - watery ghosts dragging living people into a river to drown is creepy enough.
  • Honestly, in my opinion, nothing past "In 80% of cases, peoples of Mississauga decent have been ignored by SCP-4105-B instances." is necessary. Ending on that sentence makes it short and punchy, while still having clear narrative. As it stands, the journal entries don't do much, and ending with the second Addendum dilutes the narrative.
    • Specifically, the journal entries are a rather cliche example of "Evil colonists kill friendly Indians." It's really nothing I haven't read before.
    • The theme of violence towards indigenous people is clear enough from the Containment Procs and Description. We don't need the specific example. It can already be assumed what is causing it. Adding a line saying the events have been going on since the 1700-1800s is all you need, or keeping the line about the Royal Foundation and moving it into the description.
    • The second Addenda could be moved into the Containment Procedures, if you want to keep it.

Random nitpicks:

These early warning signs will begin roughly 3 days before the SCP-4105 event

Write out numbers less than 10.

A Temperature drop of more than 15 Degrees Celsius from projected seasonal averages.

Don't capitalize temperature. I'd recommend a change to "15° C", but in any case, don't capitalize Degrees either.

SCP-4105-A will begin flowing in the opposite direction, from south to north.

To match the tense of the other warning signs, remove "will begin" from this clause.

An Estimated ████ civilian disappearances have been attributed to SCP-4105 before containment by Her Majesty's Society for the Study of the Paranormal, a Foundation precursor organization, in 1847.

Don't capitalize estimated.
